display.purpose: Hero titles, section headings, and card titles only. Never below 1.25rem.

body.purpose: All body copy, UI text, descriptions. Greek and Russian.

label.purpose: Kickers, eyebrow text, meta labels, media frame badges. Uppercase mono.

shadows.name: shadow-ink-soft
shadows.purpose: Button rest state. Invisible transition target.

shadows.name: hero-media
shadows.purpose: Hero image frame. Deep and diffuse.

motion.name: motion-duration-default
motion.purpose: Default transition duration for most interactive elements.

motion.name: motion-duration-standard
motion.purpose: Section entrance animations and media scale transitions.

motion.name: motion-ease-default
motion.purpose: Default easing curve paired with duration tokens.

motion.name: motion-ease-out
motion.purpose: Easing for state transitions where smooth deceleration is desired.

components.name: Primary Button
components.description: Main call-to-action. Ink background, Bone 50 text, fully rounded pill.

components.name: Secondary Button
components.description: Supporting action. Transparent with Ink border, sits alongside primary without competing.

components.name: Card (Focused)
components.description: Default card density. Image above, body below, full-card click target. Lifts 2px on hover.
components.html:
Ωτορινολαρυγγολογία

Πλήρης διαγνωστική και θεραπευτική προσέγγιση για παθήσεις του αυτιού, της μύτης και του λαιμού.

Μάθετε περισσότερα →

components.name: Media Frame (Wide)
components.description: Image/video container with 16:9 ratio. Placeholder stripe pattern when no media loaded.
components.html: ΠΡΟΕΠΙΣΚΟΠΗΣΗ

components.name: Content Card (Glass)
components.description: Bordered glass-surface card for content sections. Responsive internal padding.

components.name: Inline Pill
components.description: Fully rounded border pill for filter tags and meta indicators. Border shifts to accent on hover.

components.name: Navigation Link
components.description: Animated underline link. Underline grows from 1px to 2px on hover, color shifts to Trust accent.

components.name: Section Heading
components.description: Section header with eyebrow label, display title, and optional intro text. Two-column at desktop, stacked on mobile.
components.html:
ΥΠΗΡΕΣΙΕΣ

Τι θεραπεύουμε

Εξειδικευμένη φροντίδα για όλο το φάσμα των παθήσεων του ωτορινολαρυγγολογικού συστήματος.

components.name: Hairline Grid
components.description: Shared CSS Module classes for the signature hairline-bordered grid pattern. Container gets border-left; cells get border-right + border-bottom. Components compose these and layer their own grid-template-columns and nth-child overrides.

components.name: Stripe Placeholder
components.description: Diagonal stripe pattern for empty media slots. Shared via .stripe-placeholder in shared-layout.module.css (also available as .ph-stripe in design-system.module.css). Components compose this and add their own display, dimensions, and min-height.

narrative.overview: A well-appointed consulting room: clean surfaces, intentional objects, nothing that doesn't serve the patient. The myORL design system prioritizes clarity as a form of care. Every element is placed to reduce cognitive load for anxious patients researching ENT symptoms — the interface should feel like a reassuring hand on the shoulder, not a medical textbook. The system is light-only by design. Patients and family members browse on phones and laptops at home, in waiting rooms, under office lighting. A light surface maximizes readability and signals clinical cleanliness. The palette is restrained: tinted blue-grey neutrals carrying a single blue accent at ≤10% surface coverage. Instrument Serif adds warmth and personal authority at display sizes; Source Sans 3 carries body text in both Greek and Russian with equal confidence. This system explicitly rejects SaaS-cream card farms, hospital-corporate sterility, Mediterranean-tourism warmth, and crypto-dark aggressive styling. It is neither playful nor severe. It is calm, and the calm is earned by the doctor's credentials.
keyCharacteristics: Restrained color strategy: one accent (trust blue) ≤10% of any screen
Light-only theme tuned for clinical readability
Serif display + sans body: warmth through typography, not decoration
Density system (scanning / focused / theater) controls spacing and type scale globally
Flat-by-default elevation: shadows appear only on state change
Hairline grid patterns for structured content sections
WCAG 2.2 AA with enhanced focus indicators exceeding AA visibility
Bilingual parity: Greek and Russian locales are equally complete, no cross-locale fallback

rules.name: The Restrained Rule
rules.body: Trust blue covers ≤10% of any given screen. It appears on CTAs, links, and focus rings — and on nothing else. The accent's authority comes from its scarcity.

rules.name: The No-Pure-Black Rule
rules.body: Every neutral carries a blue undertone (chroma ~0.005–0.01 in OKLCH). #000 and #fff are forbidden. Ink (#0f2a4a) is the darkest color; Bone 50 (#fbfcfe) is the lightest.

rules.name: The Glass Transparency Rule
rules.body: Glass surfaces (header, nav, megamenu) use RGBA values derived from Bone 50, not arbitrary colors. Each variant corresponds to a specific opacity calibrated to its context: general panels 82%, sticky header 88%, nav bar 94%, megamenu dropdown 96%.

rules.name: The Display Authority Rule
rules.body: Instrument Serif is reserved for hero titles, section headings, and card titles. It never appears in body copy, labels, UI chrome, or at sizes below 1.25rem.

rules.name: The Bilingual Parity Rule
rules.body: All type sizes, line heights, and container widths must work in both Greek and Russian. Greek text tends to run 15–20% longer than Russian; Russian Cyrillic glyphs have different vertical metrics and ascender/descender profiles. Test every layout in both locales.

rules.name: The Balance Rule
rules.body: Headings use text-wrap: balance; body text uses text-wrap: pretty. Overflow strategy is overflow-wrap: break-word (never anywhere) with hyphens: auto keyed to the correct lang attribute (el or ru).

rules.name: The Flat-By-Default Rule
rules.body: Surfaces are flat at rest. Shadows appear only as a response to state (hover, focus, sticky scroll, overlay). If a surface isn't interactive or positioned above the page, it doesn't cast a shadow.

rules.name: The Glass-Not-Gloss Rule
rules.body: Glass surfaces (nav, header, megamenu) use backdrop-filter: blur() to separate from page content while remaining readable. They are functional — keeping navigation accessible during scroll — not decorative. The blur value is calibrated per context: general panels and megamenu at higher blur, nav at lower blur for speed.

dos: Do use the semantic aliases (--background, --foreground, --accent, --line, --surface, --muted) in CSS Modules. Reserve palette names (--color-*) for cases where the literal color value matters.
Do use the density system (scanning / focused / theater) to control both spacing and type scale. Pick one density per section; don't mix densities within a single section.
Do keep the Trust accent to ≤10% of any screen. Its authority comes from scarcity.
Do use Instrument Serif only at display sizes (≥1.25rem) for hero titles, section headings, and card titles.
Do test every layout in both Greek and Russian. Greek runs longer; Cyrillic has different vertical metrics. Neither locale is a second-class citizen.
Do provide enhanced focus indicators: minimum 2px solid Trust outline with 2px offset, visible against both the component and the page background.
Do respect prefers-reduced-motion — skip all animations when active (use animation-duration: 0.01ms with !important, not just slower durations).
Do use overflow-wrap: break-word (never anywhere) with hyphens: auto keyed to the correct lang attribute (el or ru).

Do use CSS custom property tokens for radius (--radius-sm, --radius-md, --radius-full) and motion (--motion-duration-*, --motion-ease-*) instead of hardcoded values. All 40+ hardcoded radius values and 28 hardcoded transitions have been migrated to tokens.
donts: Don't use #000 or #fff. Ink (#0f2a4a) is the darkest color; Bone 50 (#fbfcfe) is the lightest. Every neutral carries a blue undertone.
Don't use gradient text (background-clip: text combined with a gradient). Emphasis comes from weight, size, or color — never from decorative effects.
Don't use side-stripe borders (border-left or border-right greater than 1px as a colored accent on cards, list items, or callouts). Use full borders, background tints, or leading indicators instead.
Don't use glassmorphism as decoration. Glass surfaces are functional (sticky nav, overlays, megamenu) — not a visual style to apply generically.
Don't use the hero-metric template (big number + small label + supporting stats + gradient accent). This is a medical practice site, not a SaaS dashboard.
Don't use identical card grids (same-sized cards with icon + heading + text, repeated endlessly). Vary card density, layout, and presence of media by section.
Don't use modals as the first solution. Exhaust inline expansion, accordion panels, or progressive disclosure before reaching for a modal.
Don't use disabled buttons. Links are present-and-active or absent. Redirect unavailable destinations to a status page.
Don't use bounce or elastic easing curves. Default to ease-out (simple transitions) or cubic-bezier(0.16, 1, 0.3, 1) (entrance animations).
Don't animate CSS layout properties (padding, margin, width, height). Animate transform and opacity only.
Don't use dark mode, purple gradients, neon accents, or crypto/tech aggressive styling. This is medicine — the interface reassures, it doesn't impress.
